title: Sequential Compactness in Metric Spaces: A Logical Flow
description: This flowchart illustrates the logical equivalence between sequential compactness and compactness (via the open-cover definition) in the context of metric spaces, which is a key result in analysis.
relationships: A set K is sequentially compact if every sequence in K has a convergent subsequence (limit in K).
A set K is compact if every open cover of K has a finite subcover.
In a metric space, these two properties are equivalent: K is compact IF AND ONLY IF K is sequentially compact.
